cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out between what times Andrew could have gone to the clothing store. The store was closed after 9pm, so the latest he could go is before 9pm. First, let me list out all the times Andrew was seen doing other things: - Andrew woke up at 5am. So he's up then, but that's the starting point. - William saw him playing tennis from 5am to 6am. So from 5-6am, he's at the tennis court. - Jessica saw him buying lunch at the deli from 6am to 9am. So 6-9am, he's at the deli. - Tiffany saw him taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 9am to 11am. So 9-11am, he's there. - John saw him waiting at the train station from 11am to 5pm. That's a long time, 11am to 5pm. - Sarah saw him reading at the library from 6pm to 9pm. So 6-9pm, he's at the library. The clothing store was closed after 9pm, so he must have gone before that. Now, I need to find a time when Andrew wasn't doing any of these activities. Let's check each time slot. Let me make a timeline: - 5am-6am: Tennis - 6am-9am: Deli - 9am-11am: Pisa photos - 11am-5pm: Train station - 6pm-9pm: Library So between these times, when is he free? Let's look for gaps. From 5am to 6am: busy. Then 6-9am: busy. Then 9-11am: busy. Then 11am-5pm: busy. Then 5pm to 6pm: not accounted for. Then 6-9pm: busy again. So the only time he's not doing anything is between 5pm and 6pm. Because after 5pm, from 5pm to 6pm, there's no activity mentioned. Then at 6pm he's at the library until 9pm. Also, before 5am, he was asleep. So the only possible time he could have gone to the clothing store is 5pm to 6pm. Because after that, he's at the library, and before that, he's at the train station until 5pm. Looking at the options: A) 9am-11am: He was taking photos then. So no. B) 6pm-9pm: He's at the library. No. C) 11am-5pm: He's at the train station. No. D) 5pm-6pm: Free
